subject: Add PDP Group Deploy and Delete REST API stubs
commit_message:
Add PDP Group Deploy and Delete REST API stubs

Note: this will not build until the models-pap code has been added.

Added PDP group Delete methods, with and without version.

Added checks for OK status codes.

Added topic configuration.
Still does not have the "simplified" PDP Group deploy/undeploy.
Still won't build without "Move PDP Group classes to concepts subdirectory".

Will add junit tests for code changes in another review.
Updated comments about unlocking after updates.
Made ServiceManager final.
Included topic start/stop actions.
Fixed parameter comment.
Removed unneeded parameters from json files.
Fixed argument test in shell script.
Updated licenses.

Change-Id: I6176d51918ae758e04fb68562dc9ca70534137d4
Issue-ID: POLICY-1542
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>

Patch Set 2:

Let's discuss the synchronous/asynchronous aspects at the scrum today. I had envisaged it as being an asynchronous call that once the change was ordered the REST call was done and subsequent calls got the response. Perhaps this causes difficulties for other components?

Patch Set 2:

Trying to summarize with regards to this review.   Can you let me know if my recollection is correct?

1. we said in the scrum that the packages *.model.* should be moved to policy/models repo.   

2.  I guess we need to come to an agreement in terms of sync and async as we are discussing by email, so that's still a little in flux although we are getting near.   If we go all async, wouldn't "*Response*" objects would go away?

Feedback?

> Trying to summarize with regards to this review.   Can you let me
 > know if my recollection is correct?
 > 
 > 1. we said in the scrum that the packages *.model.* should be moved

 > "*Response*" objects would go away?

 > Feedback?

Jorge:
#1 is correct - Ram has already pushed a review for that.
#2 If we go async for this particular REST API, then I agree, it seems like the standard HTTP status code & message are sufficient - no need to define a response object.  Or do we have to define something, even if it's just an empty json object? (I'm still learning the nuances of REST.)

Patch Set 2:

> > Trying to summarize with regards to this review.   Can you let me
 > > know if my recollection is correct?
 > >
 > > 1. we said in the scrum that the packages *.model.* should be
 > moved
 > > to policy/models repo.
 > >
 > > 2.  I guess we need to come to an agreement in terms of sync and
 > > async as we are discussing by email, so that's still a little in
 > > flux although we are getting near.   If we go all async, wouldn't
 > > "*Response*" objects would go away?
 > >
 > > Feedback?
 > 
 > Jorge:
 > #1 is correct - Ram has already pushed a review for that.
 > #2 If we go async for this particular REST API, then I agree, it
 > seems like the standard HTTP status code & message are sufficient -
 > no need to define a response object.  Or do we have to define
 > something, even if it's just an empty json object? (I'm still
 > learning the nuances of REST.)

As per the specs, we have method to set message & status code in case of error. But in case of success, we can only set the status code as the api for setting message is deprecated. So if we want to send some metadata/info back then we need response object.
Also, it depends upon use case as well.

HTTP Spec says no need to send json response always, use codes instead. Whereas JSON API spec says always send json response.

Hence, if we align our API's to the standard HTTP status codes then we can remove the empty response object. Otherwise we can use it as a document to refer the meaning of the response code/any metadata info. 
Choice is in our hand!

Patch Set 2:

> > > Trying to summarize with regards to this review.   Can you let
 > me
 > > > know if my recollection is correct?
 > > >
 > > > 1. we said in the scrum that the packages *.model.* should be

 > > > Feedback?
 > >
 > > Jorge:
 > > #1 is correct - Ram has already pushed a review for that.
 > > #2 If we go async for this particular REST API, then I agree, it
 > > seems like the standard HTTP status code & message are sufficient
 > -
 > > no need to define a response object.  Or do we have to define
 > > something, even if it's just an empty json object? (I'm still
 > > learning the nuances of REST.)
 > 
 > As per the specs, we have method to set message & status code in
 > case of error. But in case of success, we can only set the status
 > code as the api for setting message is deprecated. So if we want to
 > send some metadata/info back then we need response object.
 > Also, it depends upon use case as well.
 > 
 > HTTP Spec says no need to send json response always, use codes
 > instead. Whereas JSON API spec says always send json response.
 > 
 > Hence, if we align our API's to the standard HTTP status codes then
 > we can remove the empty response object. Otherwise we can use it as
 > a document to refer the meaning of the response code/any metadata
 > info.
 > Choice is in our hand!

I read through this link: https://softwareengineering.stackexchange.com/questions/211275/should-an-http-api-always-return-a-body

Seems like it might make it easier on clients if we always return a JSON object for the POST output.  In addition, we may want to include error details if there's a problem.  With that in mind, I think response object should include a single String field, "errorDetails", while using the HTTP status code to indicate success or failure.  That would be for Group deploy & delete.

I agree, let's follow the same for Group deploy, delete & state change. Other 3 (query, statistics & healthcheck) are anyway GET API's.

Patch Set 4:

what if we successfully send to some PDPs but not all of them?  Return success?  Return partial success?  I assume we should continue to send to the other PDPs.

Patch Set 4:

> what if we successfully send to some PDPs but not all of them? 
 > Return success?  Return partial success?  I assume we should
 > continue to send to the other PDPs.

I think since we PDP-messages are placed in DMaaP, it will send to all that are listening in the PDP-PAP topic.  It's just one "send" call.

PAP should eventually find out who received it and is operating with it as the PDP-STATUS messages should be sent at regular periodic intervals.

Patch Set 4:

Hi all, I really need this to continue my work on PAP.

Patch Set 4:

I do not see Policy Deploy/Undeploy

Patch Set 4:

> I do not see Policy Deploy/Undeploy

Pam, have a look at PDPGroupDeployControllerV1.java.
The deploy endpoint here is the one which will be used for both deploy/undeploy operation. It takes in a PdpGroup object which contains all the necessary details and updates the PDP accordingly.

Patch Set 4:

I don't understand that - think from the client point of view. I am CLAMP I have 2-4 Policy ID's, I have no notion of groups. I just want to call a simple API with those ID's to deploy the policies and only that. We guaranteed CLAMP that they would NOT have to worry about groups, but only policy ID's.

Patch Set 4:

> I don't understand that - think from the client point of view. I am
 > CLAMP I have 2-4 Policy ID's, I have no notion of groups. I just
 > want to call a simple API with those ID's to deploy the policies
 > and only that. We guaranteed CLAMP that they would NOT have to
 > worry about groups, but only policy ID's.
 
As per architecture wiki, this is expected from CLAMP for deploying policy. And this structure is list of PdpGroup object.

pdp_groups:
  - name: onap.pdpgroup.Monitoring
    version: 2.1.3
    description: DCAE mS Configuration Policies
    pdp_subgroups:
      - pdp_type: xacml
          policies:
            - onap.scaleout.tca

May be its better to have a quick zoom call tomorrow and go through these. Is that ok?

 > properties both for configuration, new parameter added to json but

Ram: yes, call tomorrow is fine.  1) Regarding json & properties files: given the timeframe, it's too much work to create classes for topic configuration as existing code expects property files.  Doesn't appear to be an easy way to add something like a Properties object to the existing PapParameter class.  2) Regarding new parameter added to the json, I think that was in a previous patch; that was removed in patch #6.
